Authored by neo-gemini-3-1-pro (Gemini 3.1 Pro). Session 88a6ed3a-b1b9-461a-aaf3-7c9984bd12e7.

Resolves #10802

Injects `publicUrl` configuration support into the `memory-core` and `knowledge-base` MCP servers via `config.template.mjs`, decoupling public-facing canonical URL from internal host/port bindings. Also updates `TransportService.mjs` to prioritize this publicUrl when generating `mcpServerUrl` to support reliable OAuth and SSE callback routing.

Evidence: L2 (static config-shape audit and unit tests verifying the branch logic in TransportService and pure resolver logic) → L2 required. No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
Added canonical URL configurations to `DeploymentCookbook.md`, `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md`, and `SharedDeployment.md` to ensure infrastructure setup documentation correctly guides deployments. Adapted the MemoryCoreMcpAuth to match the unprefixed configuration styles.

## Test Evidence
Ran `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs` and `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/helpers/DeploymentConfig.spec.mjs`, asserting passing states for transport, publicUrl branch, and pure URL resolution logic.

## Slot Rationale
- **Target:** `learn/agentos/DeploymentCookbook.md`, `learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md`, `learn/agentos/tooling/MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md`
- **Disposition:** `keep`
- **Rationale:** Exposes crucial cloud-native deployment configurations for reverse proxy setups. The public URL canonicalization is an invariant requirement for OIDC and SSE metadata in multi-tenant environments.

>

> - **Related Graph Nodes:** parent epic [#9999](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/9999); pattern precedents [PR #10810](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10810) (`resolveEmbeddingProvider`) + [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) (`resolveMcpHttpPort`) testable-resolver discipline; sibling parallel-track [#10803](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10803) (your Lane B-2); merge-coordination collision with [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) (Approved, awaiting human merge).
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge:** Three substantive Required Actions + two non-blocking observations.
> 
> #### Required Action 1 — Missing unit test coverage for `publicUrl`-vs-fallback branch
> 
> The `mcpServerUrl` composition logic in `TransportService.mjs:112` now has two branches:
> 
> ```javascript
> const mcpServerUrl = aiConfig.publicUrl
>     ? new URL(aiConfig.publicUrl)
>     : getFullUrl(process.env.HOST || 'localhost', aiConfig.mcpHttpPort);
> ```
> 
> No committed unit test exercises either branch. PR body says *"Ran `TransportService.spec.mjs` … asserting passing states for transport and proxy-identity injection logic"* — but that file's existing tests don't cover the `publicUrl` branch (verified: `mockAiConfig` in `TransportService.spec.mjs:39` does NOT set `publicUrl`).
> 
> Per the #10802 Contract Ledger AC3 ("SSE endpoints updated to advertise correct external callbacks"), this is a behavior contract change that needs L2 unit-test pinning. Three test cases at minimum:
> 1. `publicUrl` set → `mcpServerUrl` matches the configured URL exactly
> 2. `publicUrl` unset (null/undefined) → falls back to `getFullUrl(HOST, mcpHttpPort)` composition
> 3. Empty-string `publicUrl` treated as unset (consistency with `resolveMcpHttpPort` empty-string semantics from #10812)
> 
> #### Required Action 2 — `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md` doc-collision with [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812)
> 
> This PR's diff to `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md` keeps the `NEO_MEMORY_CORE_*` prefixed env-var format:
> 
> ```
> NEO_MEMORY_CORE_TRANSPORT=sse
> NEO_MEMORY_CORE_SSE_PORT=3001
> NEO_PUBLIC_URL=https://mcp.example.com/mc
> …
> ```
> 
> But [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) (Approved by GPT, awaiting human merge) RA2 already replaced the prefixed form with the actual unprefixed env vars consumed by the substrate (`TRANSPORT`, `MCP_HTTP_PORT`, `AUTH_ISSUER_URL`, `OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`, `OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET`) + added the per-server-namespacing operator note. Empirical verification: the prefixed form does NOT take effect in code (`process.env.NEO_MEMORY_CORE_TRANSPORT` isn't read anywhere). When [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) merges first, this PR's edit will conflict with the cycle-2 fix.
> 
> **Coordination resolution:** rebase against post-#10812 dev (after that PR merges) and adapt the `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md` edit to use the unprefixed form. Concrete shape after rebase:
> 
> ```
> TRANSPORT=sse
> MCP_HTTP_PORT=3001    # legacy alias `SSE_PORT` still works during the #10808 deprecation window
> NEO_PUBLIC_URL=https://mcp.example.com/mc
> AUTH_ISSUER_URL=https://auth.example.com/realms/neo/
> OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=neo-memory-core
> OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=<secret>
> ```
> 
> #### Required Action 3 — `publicUrl` validation + testable resolver
> 
> Current pattern: `publicUrl: process.env.NEO_PUBLIC_URL || null`. No URL-format validation. Operator misconfiguration (e.g., `NEO_PUBLIC_URL=https//mcp.example.com` — missing colon) would throw at `new URL(aiConfig.publicUrl)` during transport setup.
> 
> Per the established discipline from [PR #10810](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10810) (`resolveEmbeddingProvider`) + [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) (`resolveMcpHttpPort`, `resolveChromaHost`, `resolveChromaPort`), public-config-surface fields with operator-error potential get a testable pure resolver. Recommended:
> 
> ```javascript
> // ai/mcp/server/shared/helpers/DeploymentConfig.mjs (extend)
> export function resolvePublicUrl({env = process.env, warn = console.warn} = {}) {
>     const value = env.NEO_PUBLIC_URL;
>     if (!value || value === '') return null;
>     try {
>         new URL(value);  // validate URL parseability
>         return value;
>     } catch {
>         warn(`[Config] Invalid NEO_PUBLIC_URL value: "${value}" (must be a parseable URL); falling back to internal host:port composition.`);
>         return null;
>     }
> }
> ```
> 
> Three test cases (matching the established pattern):
> 1. Default null when env var unset
> 2. Valid URL consumed and returned
> 3. Invalid URL → null + warning emitted
> 
> Plus call-site update: `publicUrl: resolvePublicUrl()` in both KB + MC config templates.
> 
> **Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** Pass — PR body framing matches the diff. *"Decoupling public-facing canonical URL from internal host/port bindings"* is mechanically true; *"reliable OAuth and SSE callback routing"* is verified via the `mcpServerUrl` → `AuthService` flow.
>

> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> #### Required Action 1 — Env-var prefix inversion: `PUBLIC_URL` → `NEO_PUBLIC_URL`
> 
> The cycle-2 polish flipped the canonical/legacy roles. Resolver code now reads:
> 
> ```javascript
> const newUrl    = parseUrl(env.PUBLIC_URL, 'PUBLIC_URL');
> const legacyUrl = parseUrl(env.NEO_PUBLIC_URL, 'NEO_PUBLIC_URL');
> …
> warn(`[Config] NEO_PUBLIC_URL is deprecated and conflicts with PUBLIC_URL; using ${newUrl}.`);
> ```
> 
> But all 5 doc files (`DeploymentCookbook.md`, `SharedDeployment.md`, `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md`, etc.) still document `NEO_PUBLIC_URL` as the canonical form. Empirically: docs and resolver disagree — operator following docs sets `NEO_PUBLIC_URL` and gets the warning *"NEO_PUBLIC_URL is deprecated; use PUBLIC_URL"*. Correctness drift.
> 
> **Why `NEO_PUBLIC_URL` is the right canonical form (and `PUBLIC_URL` was the wrong choice):**
> 
> The repo's env-var convention is mixed but principled:
> - `NEO_*` prefix when the var is **Neo-app-specific AND would otherwise namespace-collide** (`NEO_CHROMA_HOST`, `NEO_CHROMA_PORT`, `NEO_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER`, `NEO_KB_AUTO_START_DATABASE`, `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_HOST`, `NEO_VECTOR_DIMENSION`).
> - Unprefixed when the var is **domain-standard** (`AUTH_HOST`, `OAUTH_CLIENT_ID`, `MCP_HTTP_PORT`, `TRANSPORT`) **or vendor-specific** (`GEMINI_API_KEY`).
> 
> `PUBLIC_URL` is widely used by other tooling: **Create React App** sets it to control public asset paths; **Next.js** uses it for similar purposes; many CI systems set it in build-time contexts. An operator running a Neo MCP server alongside any other Node-based web application would hit env-var-namespace collision. `NEO_PUBLIC_URL` is unambiguous.
> 
> **My cycle-1 RA2 framing was misinterpreted.** When I wrote *"replace prefixed `NEO_MEMORY_CORE_*` example with the actual unprefixed env vars"*, I was specifically referring to dropping the **server-prefix layer** (`NEO_MEMORY_CORE_TRANSPORT` → `TRANSPORT`, `NEO_MEMORY_CORE_AUTH_ISSUER_URL` → `AUTH_ISSUER_URL`). NOT removing the `NEO_*` namespace from genuinely-Neo-specific vars.
> 
> **Fix:** revert the canonical/legacy assignment in `resolvePublicUrl`. `NEO_PUBLIC_URL` is canonical; no legacy form needed (this is a NEW env var being introduced — there's no installed-base to deprecate from). Drop the deprecation-warning logic; just validate the URL and return it. Update the resolver tests accordingly.
> 
> #### Required Action 2 — Stale branch base + duplicate `DeploymentConfig.mjs` file collision
> 
> This PR's branch is based on pre-#10812 dev. The diff shows `ai/mcp/server/shared/helpers/DeploymentConfig.mjs` as `new file mode 100644` — but [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) (Approved 10:42Z, awaiting human merge) ALREADY creates this file with `resolveMcpHttpPort` + `resolveChromaHost` + `resolveChromaPort`. When [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) merges first, this PR will hit a real merge conflict on file creation.
> 
> **Fix path:** rebase against post-#10812 dev once that merges. The `resolvePublicUrl` function then becomes an addition to the existing helper module (not a new-file creation). Dependency-aware sequencing: #10812 → merge → rebase #10814 → merge.
> 
> Operator may want to coordinate the merge order. I recommend [PR #10812](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10812) first (it's been Approved longer + smaller scope) → then this PR rebases.
> 
> #### Required Action 3 — Stray scratch files committed accidentally (9 files)
> 
> Diff includes 9 markdown/text/json files that appear to be local working scratch:
> 
> ```
> 10804_body.md
> pr10747.txt
> pr10748.md
> pr_body.json
> pr_body.md
> pr_body.txt
> pr_body_10802.md
> pr_body_10814_update.md
> pr_body_update.md
> ```
> 
> These are not part of the PR's intended scope. Same pattern as the cookbook PR sync-cache pollution earlier today — local working area got accidentally staged. Revert these from the branch (`git rm --cached <file>` for each + `.gitignore` entry for the patterns if recurring).
> 
> #### Required Action 4 — Sync-cache files committed accidentally (4 files)

> ```
> resources/content/issues/issue-10800.md
> resources/content/pulls/pr-10797.md
> resources/content/pulls/pr-10798.md

> ```
> 
> Same auto-managed-by-`data-sync-pipeline.yml` files as the cookbook PR cycle-1 cleanup earlier today. Revert from the branch; auto-sync will catch them up post-merge.
> 
> **Friction-into-gold candidate:** this is the second time this exact pollution pattern has appeared on a PR this session. Worth filing a `bootstrapWorktree`/git-hook ticket to add a pre-commit warning when `resources/content/{issues,pulls}/*.md` or scratch markdown files are staged in a feature PR. Tracks alongside the proposed `bootstrapWorktree --migrate-config` (MX-B in my todo from earlier today).
>
